You come across an ancient stone statue of King Arthur holding his legendary sword Justice.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Gestur Ironarm, a famous dwarven Rune Touched who fought a dragon long ago.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Bridgette Silverbow, a famous elven Rune Touched said to have wielded a legendary bow made of silver.
You come across an ancient stone statue of Merlin the Wizard, a famous Rune Touched said to have learnt magic in the earliest ages of the world. He was an advisor to King Arthur.
You come across an ancient stone statue depicting four heroes fighting a dragon.
You come across an ancient stone statue half buried in the ground. What you can make out looks like a fox.

A travelling bard is performing in a meadow for a crowd of people, you wander in and listen. They're telling the tale of King Arthur and how he slew the Dragon. The story finishes with how Arthur struck his sword into a stone far to the north of here.

You come across a rock with a strange glyph carved into it which looks almost like a rune. It glows as you approach.

Success: You recognise this as the mark of the Arcturian Society. A group of Rune Touched who teach Rune Magic.
Failure: It looks a bit dangerous.
You encounter a fallen cart as you travel through the forests. It seems to have been attacked, but there's no signs of people here.
Travelling through the forest you spot a large red dog chasing a squirrel. Its quite a bizarre scene.
Whilst traveling you spot a majestic stag standing on a hill.
You hear mooing in the distance as you approach it's a herd of cows passing by.

You notice a worn piece of paper caught on a branch, fluttering lightly in the wind. Curious, you reach out to pull it free and see what’s drawn on it.

Success: You carefully pull the paper loose and unfold it. On it is a sketch of someone who looks exactly like you.
Failure: You tug too hard and the fragile paper tears apart in your hands before you can see what was drawn on it.

You notice a perfectly shaped maple leaf pattern resting on a rock. Looking closer, something about it feels slightly… alive.

Success: You recognize the pattern as a camouflaged spider and wisely avoid touching it.
Failure: You reach out to touch the 'leaf' it suddenly moves! A camouflaged spider bites you for poison damage.

You notice a small loris staring directly at you from a tree branch. Suddenly it opens its mouth and releases a piercing, unnatural scream.

Success: You brace yourself and withstand the disturbing cry without harm.
Failure: The loris' scream pierces your mind and you clutch your head as it overwhelms you for psychic damage!
The wind rushes through the trees around you, bringing with it exciting sounds and smells.
You come across a sickly looking tree, coverred in decay and its leaves motley shades of red, purple and brown.
You spot two hunters standing on a hilltop, looking towards the sun with their arms outraised.
You encounter a peculiar individual, dressed flamboyantly in multi colored clothing. They laugh auspiciously and offer to sell you a +1 Shortsword for 50 Runes. Do you accept?
You see a tall oak tree leaning at an awkward angle. It'll fall soon you think to yourself as you pass it by.
A dog comes running up to you, wagging its tail!
A brisk wind rushes past you stirring up the leaves on the trees around you and creating an ambient rustling noise.
You come across a clearing in the woods, a soft grass filled meadow scattered with a few flowers. This might be a nice place to rest...
You spot a gruff dwarf driving a carriage of horses through the forest. He calls out to you and asks if you want a lift back to Base Camp?
You come across a band of hunters, laughing amongst themselves as they come back from a successful hunt.
You hear the sounds of birdsongs coming from further ahead in the woods.
There's nobody else around but you get the feeling something is watching you.
You spot glowing runes hanging in the air which lead you to an old campsite. This might be a good place to rest...
An orange fox with a white tipped tail and chest comes rushing out of the foliage next to you, a field mouse hanging limp in its mouth.
You come across a weathered stone shrine covered in moss. You can't make out which deity it was made out to but it leaves you with a sense of peace.
You hear the long echoing call of a wolf coming from somewhere deeper in the woods.
You spot a cloud that looks like a sword.
You spot a cloud that looks like a bird flying.
You spot a cloud that looks like a hand.
A hunter awkwardly approaches you and explains that they got lost. They ask you to lead them back to town.
You come across a secluded small thatched cottage. You can smell apple pie aromas wafting through the partially open window.
You overhear the sounds of two people talking loud enough to echo coming from somewhere deeper in the woods. 'He was caught doing what...?'
You come across a sturdy wooden bridge which crosses over a stream. You silently thank the bridge builder as you cross over.
You come across the overgrown moss-covered remnants of an abandoned cart.
Coming to the top of a hills ridge, you are presented with a grand view over the woods. Conveniently someone has placed some logs nearby so you might relax as you look out over the forest.

You come across a ramshackle campsite in a secluded forest clearing. A curious halfling ranger is watching you from the camps edge, whilst behind them a human fighter and an elf wizard are cooking dinner.
A suspicious looking individual tries to sneak up on you but trip and faceplant on the ground. They're soo embarrassed they run away.
You come across an old oak tree standing at the top of a hills crest. The tree must be ancient from how weathered and twisted it looked.
You travel through the woods without anything to bother you. You pass the time in peaceful contemplation.
You spot a snake laying on the ground ahead of you. You can't tell if it's waiting in ambush or sleeping, but either way you don't risk it and walk around the snake.
You come across a section of the woods with a really awful smell. There must be something disgusting around here.
You come across a recently fallen tree. It looks like it fell naturally, the tree stump looks all torn.
A couple of elves pass you by, one of them carrying a basket of apples.
A couple of dwarves pass you by, one of them carrying a basket of garlic cloves.
You come across a secluded pond where you can stop and `!fish forest`.

You come across a secluded rocky part of the forest.

Success: You could probably try some mining here. You can now use `!mine forest`.
Failure: It looks a bit dangerous.

The songs of various birds ring through the trees around you.

Success: You recognise the birds by their songs and can spot them in the trees.
Failure: It sounds pretty.

You come across some wild fruit!

Success: You pick a few apples!
Failure: You can't reach the apples.

You come across some wild fruit!

Success: You pick a few pears!
Failure: You can't reach the apples.

You come across some wild vegetables!

Success: You pick a few onions!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across some wild berries!

Success: You pick a few berries!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across some wild berries!

Success: You pick a few blackberries!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across some wild berries!

Success: You pick a few strawberries!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across some wild vegetables!

Success: You pick a few potatoes!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across some wild vegetables!

Success: You pick a few totatoes!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any edible food.

You come across a honey bee hive!

Success: You carefully take some honey!
Failure: Despite your efforts the bees fight you off!

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few garlic cloves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few mint leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some wild herbs!

Success: You pick a few arnica leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

A curious rat pauses to sniff the air as it runs passed you. After it runs off you can see a rune floating where it had stopped to sniff.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A fluffy orange fox creeps up on a field mouse, it pounces and catches the mouse before running off into the forest. There are a few runes floating in the area where it had pounced.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A trail of glimmering golden runes leads you down a long forgotten path, at the end there's a sweeping view over the forest.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A gnarled and twisted old tree stands in solitude at the top of a ridge. Climbing up to investigate you find some natural green rune.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

Red golden runes decorate a small bush making it look like it has berries.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

An adventurer in plate armor with a hefty greatsword passes you by on the road, they stop to ask you if you are rune touched before offering you a few Runes to aid you on your journey.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.
A narrow stream cuts across your path, its bed lined with small smooth pebbles.
Among the roots and brambles you spot rusted blades and broken shields, long claimed by the forest floor. Perhaps a battle was fought here long ago.
Wind-torn pages flutter from a hollow oak—sketches of blink dogs, great spiders, and one eerily calm owlbear. A scribbled margin says fuller lists circulate in print; you could try `!library beasts`.
A water-stained leaflet wedged under bark warns of Oakwood revels: when to bow, when to bolt, and which compliments bite back. It reads like half etiquette, half threat—follow up with `!library fey`.
Children's rhymes about foxes and talking trees are chalked on a flat stone, as if copied from a hearth book. The verses trail off; matching tales might sit on a shelf somewhere—`!library tales`.
A hunter passing by stops to say hello. He offers over some snacks he brought with him from home.
You come across some arnica herbs, which you know can be used to make a simple healing poultice.
You come across an apple tree, with delicious ripe apples hanging from its branches.
You stop along your jouney to eat some travel snacks.
You come across a hidden path that leads down to a crystal clear pond. The waters feel soo refreshing.
As you make your way through the foliage a grass snake takes the opportunity to leap out and bite you.
You footing slips as you climb uphill causing you to tumble and fall.
You accidentally trigger an animal trap.
You accidentally knock a hornets nest and get stung.
You step on a tripwire trap and get stung by darts.

You stepped on a tripwire, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You nimbly dodge out of the way.
Failure: You get stung by darts for piercing damage.

You stepped on a magic glyph, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You nimbly dodge out of the way.
Failure: You get caught in a magical explosions for force damage.

A boar comes rushing towards you trampling through the woods, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You catch it mid charge and push it aside.
Failure: You get trampled for bludgeoning damage.

The land uphill from you begins to shift before it starts tumbling towards you, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You brace yourself taking cover behind a large rock.
Failure: You get caught in the landslide and fall taking bludgeoning damage.

The air smells nice, fresh, fragrant, sweet... too sweet, you only have a moment to react.

Success: You brace yourself holding your breath so you don't inhale anymore toxic spores.
Failure: You realise they're toxic spores too late and take poison damage.

You see a goldish yellow glint reflecting off the ground ahead.

Success: You find 1d10 gp on the ground.
Failure: You lose sight of the glint.
Someone dropped a few coins on the ground here. Finders keepers.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Aid within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Cure Wounds within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Magic Missile within.
Failure: It's empty.
You come across a perfect ring of pale mushrooms growing in a clearing. The air within the ring feels oddly still.
A section of the forest stands blackened and silent, trees reduced to charcoal husks. At the centre, a single sapling grows vibrant and green.
Bundles of twigs, bones, and feathers hang from the branches overhead, swaying gently despite the lack of wind.
You notice a line of knee-high stones carved with faint runes stretching off between the trees, marking some long-forgotten boundary.
Half swallowed by ivy and moss, the remains of a wooden watchtower lean precariously against a large pine. Whoever built it once kept careful watch over these woods.
You find a campsite with bedrolls still laid out and a cold firepit filled with rainwater. There are no signs of a struggle—just absence.
The bark of a broad oak has grown in such a way that it resembles dozens of distorted faces, each one stretched in a silent expression.
Caught high in the branches of a tree is a torn and faded banner bearing a crest you don't recognise.
You step into a clearing where no birds sing and no insects buzz. Even your own footsteps seem muted against the grass.
A circle of standing stones rises from the earth, each one etched with weather-worn symbols and aligned carefully with the sky.
You pass beneath a great willow tree. You wonder how long the tree has been growing here.
As dusk begins to fall you glimpse a warm lantern light moving between the trees in the distance.
You intersect with the faint cobbled remains of an old road, nearly consumed by roots and soil, leading deeper into the forest.
Half embedded in the dirt at the base of a tree is an old iron helm split cleanly down the middle.
Pressed deep into the mud ahead are enormous humanoid footprints, spaced far apart as if their maker covered ground effortlessly.
A sudden gust stirs the canopy above and for a brief moment the rustling leaves sound almost like hushed voices forming words.
You find a small wooden shrine nailed to a tree, filled with trinkets, copper coins, and carved animal figurines left by passing travelers.
Sunlight breaks perfectly through the canopy into a small glade, illuminating it in warm gold while the rest of the forest remains in shadow.
Slumped against a hillside rests the inert form of a stone golem, its chest cavity cracked open and overgrown with moss.
The ground is littered with feathers of various sizes and colours, yet there is no carcass to be found.
You notice a simple wooden marker at the base of a tree. The name carved into it has long since weathered away.
A sudden burst of distant laughter rings through the trees. When you stop to listen, it does not come again.
One tree along your path appears petrified, its bark smooth and reflective like dark glass.
A hollow tree trunk glows faintly from within, like a lantern lit by unseen hands.
You find a rusted compass nailed to a tree, its needle spinning endlessly.
A line of bright feathers leads off the path before abruptly stopping.
Dozens of names are carved into a fallen log, some scratched out violently.
A single worn leather boot sits in the middle of the path.
Golden sap drips slowly from a wounded tree, glistening in the light.
Someone started building a shelter here but abandoned it halfway through.
A cluster of stones emits a low humming sound as you pass.
A perfectly preserved bird skeleton hangs tangled in branches.
A patch of moss forms a strangely comfortable natural seat.
A moss-covered stone wall cuts through the forest, long forgotten.
You spot the entrance to a fox den tucked beneath roots.
You swear you hear distant music drifting between the trees.
Small stones are carefully stacked into delicate towers.
Black charcoal symbols smear across several tree trunks.
A tree has been cleanly split down the middle by lightning.
Ashes remain from an old fire, long cold.
Tiny beetles glow faintly as they crawl across the forest floor.
Your footsteps echo strangely in this part of the forest.
All the trees here bend slightly in the same direction.
Roots form a natural archway over the path.
Deep claw marks scar the bark of nearby trees.
A small circle of ground is scorched black.
Thick webs hang between trees like curtains.
The air suddenly grows cold despite the sunlight.
A simple ring lies half-buried in the dirt.
Improvised chimes hang from branches, softly clinking.
An arrow sticks out of a tree trunk at head height.
A stone head lies cracked among roots.
A narrow fork in the path leads into darker woods.
A bird nest lies shattered on the ground.
A frayed rope dangles from a branch.
Fresh footprints lead off into thick brush.
A dense fog rolls briefly through the trees.
Knots in a tree resemble watching eyes.
A heavy branch creaks overhead ominously.
A rusted sword lies embedded in a stump.
You find a small rocky overhang dripping with water.
A faint glow flickers deep in the woods before vanishing.
A pile of small animal bones rests near a tree.
A shredded cloak catches on thorns.
A hollow tree looks like it once hid something.
A signpost points in directions that no longer exist.
A tree's bark twists into a natural spiral.
Pine cones suddenly fall around you.
A barely visible path cuts away from the main trail.
A carved stone bench sits alone.
Dark feathers scatter across the ground.
A tree bears a long, healed scar.
A wide patch of mushrooms spreads in a circle.
A broken cart wheel leans against a tree.
A wooden fence has been swallowed by vines.
A faint howl echoes far away.
The ground here looks recently disturbed.
Sticky sap forms a shape like a handprint.

You notice a faint symbol scratched into bark.

Success: You identify it as a ranger trail mark.
Failure: It seems meaningless.

Tracks cross your path in strange patterns.

Success: You determine their direction and origin.
Failure: You can't make sense of them.

A rustling sound nearby puts you on edge.

Success: You pinpoint the harmless source.
Failure: You remain uneasy.

Signs of a campsite catch your eye.

Success: You reconstruct what happened here.
Failure: You can't tell anything useful.

You hear an unfamiliar animal call.

Success: You recognise it immediately.
Failure: It remains a mystery.
A distant rhythmic drumming echoes through the trees, though you can't tell its source.
Several snapped arrows lie scattered along the forest floor.
You discover a short set of stone steps leading nowhere, now thick with moss.
The delicate skeleton of a bird rests undisturbed on a stump.
Soft chiming sounds drift from wooden charms hanging unseen among the trees.
A worn backpack lies against a tree, its contents long since taken.
Initials have been carved into the bark of a tree, faded with age.
A faint bioluminescent glow pulses from fungi growing at the base of a tree.
Small animal bones are scattered across the ground here.
Several young trees are bent unnaturally in the same direction.
A perfectly still pond reflects the canopy like a mirror.
A strip of fabric flutters from a branch, caught long ago.
The forest grows suddenly quiet as you pass through this area.
A bird's nest lies shattered on the ground below its tree.
A small cracked bell hangs from a tree branch.
Long claw marks gouge deep into the bark of nearby trees.
A cold pile of ash marks where a fire once burned.
A hollow log emits a faint whistling sound as wind passes through it.
Old trail markers have been painted onto stones, barely visible now.
A single beam of sunlight cuts dramatically through the canopy.

You hear a crack above you.

Success: You leap aside just in time.
Failure: A heavy branch crashes down onto you for bludgeoning damage.

You step into a patch of dense undergrowth.

Success: You carefully avoid the worst of it.
Failure: Sharp thorns tear at you for piercing damage.

You disturb a cluster of puffball fungi.

Success: You hold your breath and avoid inhaling them.
Failure: You inhale toxic spores and take poison damage.

The ground beneath you shifts suddenly.

Success: You regain your footing.
Failure: You slip and fall taking bludgeoning damage.

A deer bursts from the bushes toward you.

Success: You dodge out of its path.
Failure: It collides with you dealing bludgeoning damage.

You fail to notice a low hanging branch.

Success: You duck just in time.
Failure: It strikes your head for bludgeoning damage.

You step onto an anthill.

Success: You brush them off quickly.
Failure: The ants swarm and bite you for piercing damage.

You step onto slick moss covering a rock.

Success: You keep your balance.
Failure: You slip and hit the ground for bludgeoning damage.

You brush against unfamiliar leaves.

Success: You avoid any reaction.
Failure: Your skin burns and itches taking poison damage.

The ground ahead suddenly dips.

Success: You stop just in time.
Failure: You tumble down taking bludgeoning damage.

You spot a cluster of berries near a fallen log.

Success: You gather a handful of berries.
Failure: They are mostly spoiled.

You smell fresh mint nearby.

Success: You gather several mint leaves.
Failure: You fail to locate the source.

You find a hive tucked into a tree hollow.

Success: You carefully collect some honey.
Failure: The bees force you away.

A small apple tree grows in a clearing.

Success: You gather several apples.
Failure: They are out of reach.

You smell garlic growing wild nearby.

Success: You pull several cloves from the ground.
Failure: You cannot find the bulbs.

You spot arnica flowers in a meadow.

Success: You gather several leaves.
Failure: They are too sparse to collect.

A pear tree stands alone.

Success: You knock down several pears.
Failure: You fail to hit any fruit.

Wild onions grow here.

Success: You dig up several bulbs.
Failure: They are too small to use.

Strawberries grow along the path.

Success: You gather a few ripe ones.
Failure: They are unripe.

Blackberries grow in thick brambles.

Success: You collect several berries.
Failure: The thorns stop you.

A faint rune flickers in and out of existence near a tree.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

You uncover a rune half buried beneath leaves.

A refreshing breeze restores your energy.
You drink from a clean forest stream.
You take a moment to rest under a tree.
The peaceful forest soothes your mind.
Sunlight warms you through the canopy.
You find a small pouch of coins hidden under leaves.
Coins lie scattered where someone once rested.
You uncover a hidden stash of coins.
Coins have been left at the base of a tree.
A few coins lie forgotten on the path.
A hidden snare or terrain hazard signals an ambush by Owlbear.

An owls hoot catches your attention. You're not sure why but it unsettles you somehow.

Success: You spot an owlbear feather on the ground alerting you to its presence in the area. You wisely choose to avoid it.
Failure: It's beyond you. As you proceed further ahead an owlbear lunges at you out of the foliage. It has you **grappled**!

As you make your way through the forest, you keep an eye open for the signs of any danger

Success: You spot a snake sleeping in the grass ahead and avoid disturbing it.
Failure: The snake on the ground ahead blends perfectly into the grass and you accidentally step on it.

Hostile creatures use the terrain for an ambush: Wolf, Dire Wolf.

An odd mark on the trunk of a tree catches your attention. Something hit the tree.

Success: You spot an arrow on the ground nearby. The clear sign of bandits in the area. You wisely choose to avoid them.

Failure: Most of the fruit is rotten or worm-eaten.

You spot a tall pear tree swaying gently in the breeze.

Success: You knock down a few ripe pears with a thrown stick.
Failure: Your throws miss and the pears remain stubbornly out of reach.

A pear tree grows beside a shallow stream.

Success: You wade carefully and gather several low hanging pears.
Failure: You slip on wet stones and give up the attempt.

You notice the telltale green shoots of wild onions poking through the soil.

Success: You dig carefully and pull free several usable onions.
Failure: You mistake bitter roots for edible ones and find nothing worthwhile.

A patch of disturbed soil suggests edible bulbs beneath.

Success: You unearth a handful of decent onions.
Failure: The bulbs are too small or spoiled to take.

You find a low bush dotted with small red berries.

Success: You gather the ripest berries into your pouch.
Failure: You hesitate, unsure which are safe, and leave them be.

A cluster of berry bushes grows in a sunny clearing.

Success: You quickly collect a modest handful of berries.
Failure: Thorns and insects drive you off before you gather any.

Thick brambles heavy with blackberries block part of the trail.

Success: You brave the thorns and pick several ripe blackberries.
Failure: The brambles prove too dense to harvest safely.

You find a sheltered patch of glossy blackberries.

Success: You select only the darkest and sweetest berries.
Failure: Most of them are still unripe.

Wild strawberries spread across a sunny bank.

Success: You kneel and gather the ripest strawberries.
Failure: You find only small, sour fruit not worth taking.

You catch the scent of strawberries on the breeze.

Success: Tracking the scent, you gather a few bright red berries.
Failure: The scent fades and you cannot locate the patch again.

You notice sturdy leafy plants suggesting wild potatoes below.

Success: You dig and uncover several solid potatoes.
Failure: You dig for some time but find only stones and roots.

A patch of uneven earth hints at buried vegetables.

Success: You unearth a few usable potatoes.
Failure: The soil is too compacted to dig effectively.

You find wild tomato vines climbing a fallen log.

Success: You pluck a few ripe tomatoes from the vine.
Failure: Most of the tomatoes are split or overripe.

Bright red tomatoes peek out from tangled foliage.

Success: You carefully harvest the firmest tomatoes.
Failure: You disturb the vines and spoil the fruit.

High in a hollow tree you hear the buzzing of bees.

Success: You smoke the hive gently and take some honey.
Failure: The bees swarm aggressively and you retreat empty-handed.

You discover a broken hive dripping with honey.

Success: You salvage what honey remains before the insects return.
Failure: Ants and bees have already claimed it all.

You recognise the sharp scent of wild garlic nearby.

Success: You harvest several firm garlic cloves.
Failure: You mistake similar leaves and gather nothing edible.

Clusters of wild garlic grow in the shade of an oak.

Success: You pull up a few usable garlic bulbs.
Failure: The bulbs crumble apart in your hands.

A refreshing scent leads you to a patch of wild mint.

Success: You gather a bundle of fragrant mint leaves.
Failure: The patch is sparse and not worth harvesting.

Mint grows thick along the banks of a stream.

Success: You cut several sprigs of healthy mint.
Failure: The plants are waterlogged and unusable.

You spot the distinctive yellow flowers of arnica in a meadow.

Success: You carefully collect several arnica leaves.
Failure: You cannot find enough usable leaves to gather.

A rocky hillside hosts a few hardy arnica plants.

Success: You climb and harvest what leaves you can.
Failure: The climb proves too treacherous to gather safely.

You come across an oak tree!

Success: You make short work of the tree!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any useable lumber.

You come across a birch tree!

Success: You make short work of the tree!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any useable lumber.

You come across a willow tree!

Success: You make short work of the tree!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any useable lumber.

You come across a chestnut tree!

Success: You make short work of the tree!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any useable lumber.

You come across a hazelnut tree!

Success: You make short work of the tree!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest any useable lumber.

You find a massive ancient oak with a straight, sturdy trunk.

Success: With careful cuts you bring it down and salvage solid oak lumber.
Failure: The oak's dense wood dulls your tools before you can harvest anything useful.

A lightning-struck oak stands partially split but still upright.

Success: You prise away the intact sections for quality lumber.
Failure: The fractured wood splinters too badly to recover.

A young oak grove grows close together on a ridge.

Success: You selectively fell one and process it into usable beams.
Failure: The uneven ground makes proper cutting impossible.

A stand of tall white-barked birch trees sways gently in the breeze.

Success: You fell a straight birch and cut clean lengths of pale lumber.
Failure: The trunks prove thinner than expected and yield nothing worthwhile.

You find a fallen birch already stripped of branches.

Success: You trim it down into workable birch planks.
Failure: Rot has set in and the wood crumbles under your blade.

Several young birch trees cluster near a stream.

Success: You harvest one cleanly for light, flexible lumber.
Failure: The damp wood proves too warped to salvage.

A willow tree drapes its long branches over a pond.

Success: You cut and bundle flexible willow wood for later use.
Failure: The waterlogged wood splits and cannot be used.

You find a thick-trunked willow growing on firm ground.

Success: You carefully harvest usable lengths of willow lumber.
Failure: The fibrous grain resists your cuts and yields nothing.

A storm has brought down a mature willow across your path.

Success: You take advantage and process the fallen trunk into lumber.
Failure: Most of the wood is twisted and unsuitable.

You discover a broad chestnut tree with a straight trunk.

Success: You harvest sturdy chestnut lumber from its core.
Failure: The wood is riddled with knots and unusable.

A chestnut tree stands at the edge of a clearing.

Success: You fell it cleanly and salvage workable timber.
Failure: Your cuts go awry and waste the best sections.

You find a partially fallen chestnut leaning against another tree.

Success: You separate the intact trunk and gather usable lumber.
Failure: The leaning trunk shifts dangerously and you abandon the effort.

A cluster of hazelnut trees grows thick along the trail.

Success: You cut several straight sections suitable for light lumber.
Failure: The trunks are too thin to provide usable wood.

You find a mature hazelnut tree with a surprisingly sturdy trunk.

Success: You process it into smaller but solid beams.
Failure: Hidden rot ruins the wood once you begin cutting.

A wind-damaged hazelnut tree creaks ominously.

Success: You carefully fell it and gather salvageable lumber.
Failure: The unstable tree makes harvesting too dangerous.
The wind rushes across the water, bringing with it exciting sounds and smells.
You feel you'd have more luck fishing at a river.
You feel you'd have more luck fishing at the sea.
It feels like ages since you caught anything.
Another person comes down to the water to fish a short distance from you.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a carp!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a snapper!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a minnow!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a trout!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a bass!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.
